body: Routing to the team for assistance: @ArthurMa1978

## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member best able to assist.

**Summary:** You're suggesting that the `Azure.ResourceManager.KeyVault` 1.4.0 release notes should document a behavioral change: the new `2026-02-01` API version defaults to RBAC-based access control instead of vault access policies (the previous default). This is a documentation/changelog improvement request.

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Prediction</summary>

- **Category:** `Mgmt` — The affected package is `Azure.ResourceManager.KeyVault`, which is a management-plane SDK (Azure.ResourceManager.* prefix).
- **Service:** `KeyVault` — Matches the Key Vault service SDK at `/sdk/keyvault/`.
- **Confidence:** High — package name and service are unambiguous.

</details>

<details>
<summary>🔀 Routing</summary>

CODEOWNERS bottom-to-top scan matched the `%Mgmt` catch-all entry with AzureSdkOwner `@ArthurMa1978`. No more specific `%KeyVault %Mgmt` entry exists in CODEOWNERS.

</details>

<details>
<summary>💡 Suggestions</summary>

- The behavior change (RBAC as default vs. access policies) is driven by the Azure Key Vault REST API `2026-02-01` change. A note in the [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/keyvault/Azure.ResourceManager.KeyVault/CHANGELOG.md) for version 1.4.0 under a "Breaking Changes" or "Notable Changes" section would be helpful.
- Relevant reference: [Azure Key Vault access control defaults](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/key-vault/general/access-control-default).

</details>

<details>
<summary>🔍 Similar Issues</summary>

No closely matching open duplicate issues were found.

</details>
